data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Steel Dynamics (STLD) After A 68% One Year Rally?
data.description:
• If you are asking whether Steel Dynamics at US$194.11 still offers value or is pricing in too much optimism, the starting point is to look closely at how its current share price lines up with different valuation checks.
  • The stock has returned 3.5% over the past 7 days, 11.4% over 30 days, 10.3% year to date, and 67.8% over 1 year, which naturally raises questions about what is already reflected in the price.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Steel Dynamics as a key name in US-listed steel producers, with attention on how its position in the sector relates to industrial activity and capital spending. Commentary around the stock often highlights how investors are weighing sector specific factors against broader materials sentiment when interpreting these returns.
  • On Simply Wall St's 6 point valuation framework, Steel Dynamics records a value score of 3 out of 6. The next sections will break down what that means across different methods and will finish with a broader way to think about value that goes beyond a single score.

Find out why Steel Dynamics's 67.8% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Steel Dynamics Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes expected future cash flows, then discounts them back to today to estimate what the business might be worth right now. It is essentially a way of translating future cash generation into a single present value figure.

For Steel Dynamics, Simply Wall St applies a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model using cash flow projections expressed in US$. The latest twelve month free cash flow is reported as a loss of about $42.3 million, so the model leans heavily on expected future free cash flow rather than recent figures. Analyst and extrapolated estimates suggest free cash flow reaching about $3.3 billion in 2035, with intermediate projections such as $2.0 billion in 2026 and $2.6 billion in 2028, all discounted back to today using the DCF framework.

On this basis, the estimated intrinsic value comes out at roughly $310.17 per share, compared with the current share price of about $194.11. That implies a 37.4% discount, which indicates that Steel Dynamics is trading below this model-based estimate of fair value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Steel Dynamics is undervalued by 37.4%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 60 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Steel Dynamics.

Approach 2: Steel Dynamics Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful guide because it links what you pay for the stock to the earnings the business is currently generating. The higher the expected earnings growth and the lower the perceived risk, the more investors are often willing to pay in terms of a higher P/E multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually supports a lower, more conservative P/E.

Steel Dynamics currently trades on a P/E of 23.7x. This sits above the peer group average of 20.2x and also above the broader Metals and Mining industry average of 22.9x. This suggests the market is applying a somewhat richer earnings multiple than these simple benchmarks. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Steel Dynamics is 27.4x, which is the P/E level implied by its model after factoring in elements such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market size and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a straight peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for those underlying differences rather than assuming all companies deserve the same multiple. Since the Fair Ratio of 27.4x is above the current P/E of 23.7x, this approach suggests the shares may be trading below that model based earnings multiple.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Steel Dynamics Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives on Simply Wall St let you set out your story for Steel Dynamics, link that story to your own revenue, earnings and margin estimates, convert those into a Fair Value, and then compare that Fair Value with the current price in a simple tool on the Community page that updates when new information such as earnings or tariff news arrives. For example, one investor might build a narrative around strong infrastructure demand, 6.8% forecast revenue growth, an 8.3% profit margin and a Fair Value close to the current analyst consensus of US$188.25. Another might focus on risks like industry overcapacity and pressure from new investments and arrive at a lower Fair Value, with each Narrative giving a clear, price-anchored framework for deciding whether the stock currently fits their own view.

data.title: Scotiabank’s Scotia Intelligence Puts AI And Data Ethics At The Core
data.description:
• Bank of Nova Scotia has launched "Scotia Intelligence", an enterprise AI platform designed to support employees and clients globally.
  • The platform introduces assistive and autonomous AI tools for tasks such as client query support, commercial email workflows, and predictive banking prompts.
  • Scotia Intelligence includes a strong focus on data governance and ethics, with implementations that have already received industry recognition.

For investors watching TSX:BNS, Scotia Intelligence adds a new layer to the story around technology use inside a large Canadian bank. The shares recently closed at CA$103.43, with a value score of 4 and a 1 year return of 66.3%. Over the past 3 years the return stands at 78.6%, while the 5 year figure is 76.8%.

This AI rollout is aimed at integrating tools directly into daily workflows and coding processes, rather than existing as a separate app. For readers tracking how large banks are using AI, the emphasis on governance, data ethics and real operational use cases makes Scotia Intelligence an important development to watch over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Bank of Nova Scotia by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Bank of Nova Scotia.

📰 Beyond the headline: 1 risk and 4 things going right for Bank of Nova Scotia that every investor should see.

For Bank of Nova Scotia, Scotia Intelligence looks less like a flashy front end and more like core plumbing for how the bank works day to day. AI is already handling over 40% of contact centre queries and routing about 90% of commercial emails, which directly targets two big pressure points for large banks: operating costs and service speed. If these tools keep freeing up staff time for higher value work, that could support efforts to improve efficiency, client retention and cross-selling, areas where Scotiabank competes with peers such as Royal Bank of Canada, TD Bank and Bank of Montreal.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Bank of Nova Scotia Narrative

  • The narrative highlights digital banking and AI driven solutions as a way to improve efficiency and deepen relationships, and Scotia Intelligence speaks directly to that by embedding AI into contact centres, commercial workflows and predictive prompts.
  • The narrative also flags lagging digital adoption as a concern, so execution risk remains if staff uptake is slower than planned or if client experiences do not clearly improve.
  • The dedicated data ethics framework, including mandatory training and case reviews, adds a governance angle that is not fully captured in the existing narrative but may influence how regulators and clients view Scotiabank’s AI use.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Bank of Nova Scotia to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • Heavy use of AI in client interactions and document handling raises operational and reputational risk if errors occur or models behave unpredictably, especially across multiple countries and regulations.
  • Analysts have flagged credit quality, housing exposure and digital competition as key risks, and heavy AI investment could fall short of expectations if it does not translate into better returns than peers.
  • Scotia Intelligence targets cost-to-serve and speed of response across contact centres and commercial banking, which could support better efficiency over time if volumes scale through the same infrastructure.
  • A formal data ethics program and public statement may help maintain trust with clients and regulators as AI use grows, which is important when banks increasingly rely on automated decisions and recommendations.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors may want to track how quickly Scotia Intelligence usage spreads beyond early adopters in branches and contact centres, and whether management starts quantifying productivity gains or service metrics tied to the platform. Comparisons with other large banks, such as RBC, TD or JPMorgan, can also help gauge whether Scotiabank is keeping pace on AI powered tools or simply closing a gap. Any future disclosure on AI related costs, efficiency ratios, or client satisfaction scores will be useful signals of whether this product launch is turning into a durable competitive edge or remains mostly an internal technology upgrade.

data.title: A Look At NioCorp Developments (NasdaqGM:NB) Valuation After The Traxys Elk Creek Offtake Agreement News
data.description:
NioCorp Developments (NasdaqGM:NB) recently announced a non binding term sheet with Traxys North America LLC that outlines an extensive marketing and offtake framework for critical minerals from its Elk Creek Project.

See our latest analysis for NioCorp Developments.

The Traxys term sheet arrives after a sharp shift in sentiment, with the share price up 18.44% in the last day and a 1 year total shareholder return of 55.94% despite a weaker 90 day share price return. Recent momentum appears to be rebuilding after a softer patch.

If this kind of critical minerals story has your attention, it can be useful to see what else is out there in the space and compare potential opportunities using our rare earths stock screener, starting with 30 best rare earth metal stocks

With the stock up strongly over the past year and trading at $5.91 compared with an analyst price target of $11.18, the key question is whether NioCorp remains undervalued or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Advertisement

Preferred Price to Book Multiple of 2.7x: Is It Justified?

On a P/B basis, NioCorp trades at 2.7x, which sits below both its direct peer average of 9.5x and the broader US metals and mining average of 3x.

The P/B ratio compares the company’s market value to the book value of its net assets. This can be a useful reference point for asset heavy businesses with limited current earnings. For a pre revenue, loss making developer like NioCorp, this tends to reflect how much investors are willing to pay today for its Elk Creek project and other assets relative to their current accounting value.

Given NioCorp’s lack of meaningful revenue, current net loss of $56.108m and the fact that earnings have declined by 31.3% per year over the past 5 years, the relatively low P/B multiple indicates the market is assigning a more conservative price tag than peers that already have stronger earnings profiles. At the same time, the P/B level still sits quite close to the wider industry average, which shows investors are not heavily discounting it compared with other US metals and mining names.

Against the sector, the contrast is clear. Direct peers on average trade at a P/B of 9.5x, more than three times NioCorp’s 2.7x, while the broader US metals and mining group stands at 3x. That gap to peers points to a materially lower valuation being applied to NioCorp’s balance sheet, even though it is roughly in line with the industry overall.
